data.name: Senolytics
data.description: Drugs that selectively kill senescent ('zombie') cells that accumulate with age and drive inflammation and tissue dysfunction.
data.mechanism: Dasatinib + quercetin, fisetin, navitoclax - induce apoptosis in senescent cells
data.status: Clinical trials; Unity Biotechnology's UBX1325 in Phase 2 for age-related eye disease
data.developer: Unity Biotechnology, Oisín Biotechnologies, Mayo Clinic

data.name: NAD+ Repletion
data.description: Boosting NAD+ levels (critical coenzyme that declines with age) through precursors like NMN and NR. Shown to improve mitochondrial function and metabolism in mice.
data.mechanism: NMN (nicotinamide mononucleotide) or NR (nicotinamide riboside) supplementation boosts NAD+ levels
data.status: NMN/NR supplements widely available; clinical trials ongoing; FDA challenged NMN as supplement in 2023

data.name: mTOR Inhibition (Rapamycin)
data.description: Rapamycin inhibits mTOR, a master growth regulator. In mice, rapamycin extends lifespan 25%+ even when started late in life. The most robust life-extension drug in mammals.
data.mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and increasing autophagy; mimics dietary restriction
data.status: Off-label use growing; dog longevity trials (RAPID, TRUTH); human trials planned
data.developer: ResTORbio (now part of EQRx), AgelessRx, University of Washington

data.name: Epigenetic Reprogramming (Yamanaka Factors)
data.description: Partial reprogramming using OSKM (Oct4, Sox2, Klf4, Myc) factors to reverse epigenetic age without dedifferentiating cells to pluripotency. The most exciting rejuvenation approach.
data.mechanism: Cyclic expression of Yamanaka factors resets epigenetic marks, restoring youthful gene expression patterns
data.status: Altos Labs ($3B funded); Turn Biotechnologies; in vivo mouse studies show rejuvenation of multiple tissues
data.developer: Altos Labs, Turn Bio, AgeX, Ovelle Therapeutics

data.name: Metformin (TAME Study)
data.description: The diabetes drug metformin is associated with reduced all-cause mortality in diabetics vs non-diabetics. The TAME (Targeting Aging with Metformin) study aims to prove aging can be treated as a condition.
data.mechanism: Activates AMPK, inhibits mitochondrial complex I, reduces inflammation and insulin/IGF-1 signaling
data.status: TAME study funded ($65M+); enrolling 3,000+ participants aged 65-79; results expected 2026-2028
data.developer: AFAR (American Federation for Aging Research), Nir Barzilai (Einstein)

data.name: Telomere Extension
data.description: Extending telomeres (protective chromosome caps that shorten with each cell division) to delay cellular senescence. Short telomeres are a hallmark of aging.
data.mechanism: Telomerase gene therapy (AAV-TERT) or transient mRNA delivery of TERT
data.status: BioViva CEO received telomerase gene therapy (2015, unverified); Libella Gene Therapeutics clinical trial in Colombia; no FDA-approved treatment
data.developer: BioViva, Libella Gene Therapeutics, Sierra Sciences

data.name: Growth Differentiation Factor 11 (GDF11)
data.description: A circulating protein that declines with age. Parabiosis experiments (sharing blood between young and old mice) suggested GDF11 rejuvenates heart, muscle, and brain. Results debated.
data.mechanism: Rejuvenates aged stem cells and tissue function; exact mechanism debated
data.status: Controversial - some groups couldn't replicate; Alkahest (now Grifols) developing plasma-derived therapies

data.name: Klotho Protein
data.description: A protein that declines with age. Mice with extra Klotho live 20-30% longer. Klotho enhances cognition and may protect against neurodegeneration.
data.mechanism: Regulates mineral metabolism, reduces oxidative stress, enhances synaptic plasticity and cognition
data.status: UCSF showing Klotho boosts cognition in old monkeys (2023); human trials being planned
